- [MagicNetworkMonitoringRule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magicnetworkmonitoring/magicnetworkmonitoringrule) — A Magic Network Monitoring (MNM) rule — alerts when traffic to a set of IPv4 prefixes exceeds a static threshold (`threshold`), deviates from the learned baseline (`zscore`), or matches advanced DDoS criteria (`advanced_ddos`, Magic Transit only).
- [MagicSite](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/magicsite) — A Magic WAN site — represents a physical or logical network location (typically backed by a Magic WAN Connector appliance) under which LANs, WANs, and ACLs are configured.
- [MagicSiteAcl](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/magicsiteacl) — An ACL between two LANs of a Magic WAN site — allows traffic between LAN segments behind a Magic WAN Connector (all inter-LAN traffic is denied by default).
- [MagicSiteLan](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/magicsitelan) — A LAN attached to a Magic WAN site — describes a local network segment behind a Magic WAN Connector port (VLAN, addressing, routed subnets, NAT).
- [MagicSiteWan](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/magicsitewan) — A WAN attached to a Magic WAN site — describes an uplink on a Magic WAN Connector port (addressing, VLAN, load-balancing priority). Cloudflare automatically creates IPsec tunnels over each WAN.
- [MagicStaticRoute](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magictransit/magicstaticroute) — A Magic Transit / Magic WAN static route — steers traffic for a prefix to a next-hop (usually a Magic tunnel interface address).
- [ManagedTransforms](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/managedtransforms/managedtransforms) — The managed request/response header transforms of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/managed_headers`) — a zone-scoped **singleton**: every zone always carries the full catalog of managed transforms (each with an enabled flag), so there is no create or delete on the Cloudflare side.
- [messages](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/queue/messages) — Subscribe to a Cloudflare Queue with an Effect stream handler.
- [MtlsCertificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/mtlscertificate/mtlscertificate) — An account-level Cloudflare mTLS certificate.
- [NetworkInterconnectSettings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/networkinterconnects/networkinterconnectsettings) — Account-level settings for Cloudflare Network Interconnect (CNI v2) — currently the default ASN applied to new CNI BGP configurations (`/accounts/{account_id}/cni/settings`).
- [NotificationPolicy](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/alerting/notificationpolicy) — A Cloudflare Notifications policy.
- [NotificationWebhook](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/alerting/notificationwebhook) — A Cloudflare Notifications webhook destination.
- [ObservabilityDestination](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/observabilitydestination) — A Workers observability destination — an account-level OTLP export of Workers Logs telemetry (traces, logs, or metrics) pushed to an external HTTPS collector via Logpush.
- [OnRamp](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/magiccloudnetworking/onramp) — A Magic Cloud Networking on-ramp — connects cloud VPCs/VNets to Magic WAN by provisioning VPN/Transit-Gateway constructs inside the cloud account registered via a `CloudIntegration`.
- [Organization](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/organization/organization) — A Cloudflare Organization — the hierarchical container above accounts.
- [OriginCaCertificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/origincacertificate/origincacertificate) — A Cloudflare Origin CA certificate — a free certificate signed by Cloudflare's Origin CA that encrypts traffic between Cloudflare's edge and your origin server. Origin CA certificates are only trusted by Cloudflare (not by browsers), so they are used together with proxied DNS records.
- [OriginCloudRegion](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/cache/origincloudregion) — An origin cloud-region mapping of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/cache/origin_cloud_regions`).
- [OriginPostQuantumEncryption](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/originpostquantumencryption/originpostquantumencryption) — Origin Post-Quantum Encryption for a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/cache/origin_post_quantum_encryption`).
- [OriginTlsClientAuthCertificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/origintlsclientauth/origintlsclientauthcertificate) — A zone-level Authenticated Origin Pulls (AOP) client certificate (`/zones/{zone_id}/origin_tls_client_auth`).
- [OriginTlsClientAuthHostnameAssociation](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/origintlsclientauth/origintlsclientauthhostnameassociation) — A per-hostname Authenticated Origin Pulls (AOP) association (`/zones/{zone_id}/origin_tls_client_auth/hostnames`).
- [OriginTlsClientAuthHostnameCertificate](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/origintlsclientauth/origintlsclientauthhostnamecertificate) — A per-hostname Authenticated Origin Pulls (AOP) client certificate (`/zones/{zone_id}/origin_tls_client_auth/hostnames/certificates`).
- [OriginTlsClientAuthSetting](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/origintlsclientauth/origintlsclientauthsetting) — The zone-level Authenticated Origin Pulls (AOP) toggle (`/zones/{zone_id}/origin_tls_client_auth/settings`).
- [PageRule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pagerule/pagerule) — A Cloudflare **Page Rule** — a legacy zone-level rule that matches a URL pattern (`target`) and applies a set of `actions` (cache settings, SSL mode, redirects, etc.) at a given `priority`.
- [PagesDeployment](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pages/pagesdeployment) — A direct-upload deployment on a Cloudflare Pages project.
- [PagesDomain](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pages/pagesdomain) — A custom domain attached to a Cloudflare Pages project.
- [PageShieldPolicy](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pageshield/pageshieldpolicy) — A Page Shield policy — a Content Security Policy rule (`/zones/{zone_id}/page_shield/policies`) that is applied when its expression matches a request.
- [PageShieldSettings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pageshield/pageshieldsettings) — The Page Shield configuration of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/page_shield`).
- [PagesProject](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pages/pagesproject) — A Cloudflare Pages project (direct-upload).
- [Pipeline](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pipelines/pipeline) — A Cloudflare SQL Pipeline — the transform of the Pipelines product. A pipeline is a single SQL statement that reads events from a {@link PipelineStream} and writes them to a {@link PipelineSink}, both referenced by name.
- [PipelineSink](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pipelines/pipelinesink) — A Cloudflare Pipelines sink — the destination of the Pipelines product. A SQL {@link Pipeline} reads events from a {@link PipelineStream} and writes them to a sink, which stores them in R2 either as raw files (`r2`) or as Iceberg tables via the R2 Data Catalog (`r2_data_catalog`).
- [PipelineStream](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/pipelines/pipelinestream) — A Cloudflare Pipelines stream — the ingestion endpoint of the Pipelines product. Events are sent to a stream over HTTP (and/or from Workers via a binding), transformed by a SQL {@link Pipeline}, and written to a {@link PipelineSink}.
- [Queue](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/queue/queue) — A Cloudflare Queue for reliable message passing between Workers.
- [QueueConsumer](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/queue/queueconsumer) — A Cloudflare Queue Consumer that processes messages from a Queue.
- [QueueSubscription](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/queue/queuesubscription) — A Cloudflare Queues event subscription — delivers platform events (R2 bucket events, KV namespace events, Workers Builds, Workflows, etc.) into a Queue as messages.
- [QueueWrite](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/queue/queuewrite) — Binding service that turns a {@link Queue} resource into a typed {@link WriteQueueClient} you can call from a Worker's runtime Effect.
- [R2Bucket](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/r2/r2bucket) — A Cloudflare R2 object storage bucket with S3-compatible API.
- [R2BucketEventNotification](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/r2/r2bucketeventnotification) — Event notifications for a Cloudflare R2 bucket, delivered to a Queue.
- [R2BucketSippy](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/r2/r2bucketsippy) — Sippy — incremental migration from AWS S3 or Google Cloud Storage into a Cloudflare R2 bucket.
- [R2DataCatalog](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/r2datacatalog/r2datacatalog) — Apache Iceberg data catalog attached to a Cloudflare R2 bucket.
- [RateLimit](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ratelimit/ratelimit) — A Cloudflare Rate Limit binding for counting arbitrary keys inside Workers.
- [RealtimeKitApp](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/realtimekit/realtimekitapp) — A Cloudflare RealtimeKit app — the organizational container for RealtimeKit meetings, presets, and webhooks.
- [RealtimeKitPreset](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/realtimekit/realtimekitpreset) — A Cloudflare RealtimeKit preset — a named participant role (e.g. `host`, `guest`) bundling permissions, media quality, and UI design tokens for a RealtimeKit app.
- [RealtimeKitWebhook](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/realtimekit/realtimekitwebhook) — A Cloudflare RealtimeKit webhook — receives meeting, recording, livestream, transcript, and summary events for a RealtimeKit app.
- [RegionalHostname](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/regionalhostname/regionalhostname) — A Regional Hostname restricts which Cloudflare data centers decrypt and service HTTPS traffic for a hostname (Data Localization Suite / Regional Services).
- [RegionalTieredCache](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/cache/regionaltieredcache) — The Regional Tiered Cache setting of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/cache/regional_tiered_cache`).
- [RegistrarDomain](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/registrar/registrardomain) — The mutable Cloudflare Registrar settings (`auto_renew`, `locked`, `privacy`) on a domain that is already registered with Cloudflare Registrar.
- [RiskScoringIntegration](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/riskscoring/riskscoringintegration) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust **risk scoring integration** — a Shared Signals Framework (SSF) push integration that exports user risk-score changes to a third-party tenant (currently Okta) so the IdP can react to risky behavior detected by Zero Trust.
- [RpcDurableObjectNamespace](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/rpcdurableobjectnamespace) — `RpcDurableObjectNamespace` is sugar over {@link DurableObjectNamespace} for Durable Objects whose surface is a typed Effect `RpcGroup`. The DO serves an `RpcServer.toHttpEffect(group)` on its own `fetch`, and consumers see `namespace.getByName(id)` as a typed `RpcClient` directly — no manual client wiring.
- [RpcWorker](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/rpcworker) — `RpcWorker` is a thin sugar over {@link Worker} for the common case where a worker's entire `fetch` surface is a typed Effect `RpcGroup`. It takes the rpc `schema` directly in props alongside `main`, and accepts an init Effect that returns the already-piped `RpcServer.toHttpEffect(...)`-producing Effect (no `{ fetch }` wrapper) — the wrapper plugs it into the worker's `fetch` for you.
- [Ruleset](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ruleset/ruleset) — A Cloudflare Ruleset phase entrypoint for a zone.
- [RulesetAccountEntrypoint](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ruleset/rulesetaccountentrypoint) — A Cloudflare Ruleset phase entrypoint for an account.
- [RulesList](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/rules/ruleslist) — A Cloudflare account-level List (Lists API) — a named collection of IP addresses, ASNs, hostnames, or URL redirects referenced from ruleset expressions (`ip.src in $my_list`) and Bulk Redirect rules.
- [RumRule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/rum/rumrule) — A Cloudflare Web Analytics (RUM) rule.
- [RumSite](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/rum/rumsite) — A Cloudflare Web Analytics (RUM) site.
- [SchemaValidationOperationSetting](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/schemavalidation/schemavalidationoperationsetting) — A per-operation schema validation override (`/zones/{zone_id}/schema_validation/settings/operations/{operation_id}`) — pins a mitigation action for a single API Shield operation, superseding the zone-level default just for that operation.
- [SchemaValidationSchema](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/schemavalidation/schemavalidationschema) — An OpenAPI v3 schema uploaded to a zone for API Shield schema validation (`/zones/{zone_id}/schema_validation/schemas`).
- [SchemaValidationSettings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/schemavalidation/schemavalidationsettings) — Zone-level schema validation settings (`/zones/{zone_id}/schema_validation/settings`) — the default mitigation action applied to requests that do not conform to an enabled schema, plus an optional zone-wide kill switch.
- [Secret](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/secretsstore/secret) — A single secret stored inside a Cloudflare Secrets Store.
- [SecretsStore](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/secretsstore/secretsstore) — A Cloudflare Secrets Store, a per-account container for secrets that can be bound into Workers with full redaction and audit support.
- [SecurityTxt](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/securitytxt/securitytxt) — A zone's `security.txt` file (`/zones/{zone_id}/security-center/securitytxt`), served by the Cloudflare edge at `https://<zone>/.well-known/security.txt` per RFC 9116 so security researchers know how to report vulnerabilities.
- [SendEmailBinding](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/email/sendemailbinding) — A typed runtime accessor for a Cloudflare `send_email` Worker binding.
- [Share](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/resourcesharing/share) — A Cloudflare resource share — shares account-level configuration (gateway policies, custom rulesets, IdP federation grants, …) with another account or organization.
- [ShareRecipient](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/resourcesharing/sharerecipient) — A recipient on an existing Cloudflare share — grants another account or organization access to the share's resources.
- [ShareResource](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/resourcesharing/shareresource) — A resource entry on an existing Cloudflare share — adds a shareable resource (gateway policy, custom ruleset, …) to a `Share` incrementally.
- [Silence](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/alerting/silence) — A Cloudflare Notifications silence window.
- [SmartRouting](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/argo/smartrouting) — Argo Smart Routing for a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/argo/smart_routing`).
- [SmartTieredCache](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/cache/smarttieredcache) — The Smart Tiered Cache setting of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/cache/tiered_cache_smart_topology_enable`).
- [Snippet](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/snippets/snippet) — A Cloudflare Snippet — a lightweight JavaScript module that runs on Cloudflare's edge to modify HTTP traffic for a zone.
- [SnippetRules](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/snippets/snippetrules) — The ordered list of snippet rules for a Cloudflare zone.
- [SpectrumApplication](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/spectrum/spectrumapplication) — A Cloudflare Spectrum application — DDoS protection and proxying for arbitrary TCP/UDP services (SSH, Minecraft, RDP, custom protocols), not just HTTP.
- [SpeedTestSchedule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/speed/speedtestschedule) — A recurring Cloudflare Observatory (Speed) test schedule — Cloudflare runs a Lighthouse test against a page on your zone on a `DAILY` or `WEEKLY` cadence from a region of your choice.
- [StaticSite](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/website/staticsite) — A Cloudflare Worker that serves static assets built by a shell command.
- [StreamLiveInput](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/stream/streamliveinput) — A Cloudflare Stream live input — an ingest endpoint (RTMPS/SRT/WebRTC) that accepts live video and optionally records it as a Stream video.
- [StreamLiveInputOutput](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/stream/streamliveinputoutput) — A Cloudflare Stream live input output — restreams (simulcasts) live video received by a `StreamLiveInput` to another RTMP(S) destination such as YouTube Live or Twitch.
- [StreamSigningKey](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/stream/streamsigningkey) — A Cloudflare Stream signing key — an RSA key pair used to sign viewer playback tokens for videos that require signed URLs.
- [StreamWatermark](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/stream/streamwatermark) — A Cloudflare Stream watermark profile — a PNG image stamped onto videos at upload time.
- [StreamWebhook](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/stream/streamwebhook) — The Cloudflare Stream webhook — an **account-level singleton** that receives notifications when videos finish processing or live inputs connect/disconnect.
- [SynProtectionFilter](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ddosprotection/synprotectionfilter) — An Advanced TCP Protection SYN Protection filter (Magic Transit).
- [SynProtectionRule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ddosprotection/synprotectionrule) — An Advanced TCP Protection SYN flood rule (Magic Transit).
- [TcpFlowProtectionFilter](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ddosprotection/tcpflowprotectionfilter) — An Advanced TCP Protection TCP Flow Protection filter (Magic Transit).
- [TcpFlowProtectionRule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ddosprotection/tcpflowprotectionrule) — An Advanced TCP Protection out-of-state TCP flow rule (Magic Transit).
- [TieredCaching](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/argo/tieredcaching) — Tiered Caching for a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/argo/tiered_caching`).
- [TokenConfiguration](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tokenvalidation/tokenconfiguration) — An API Shield JWT validation token configuration — the JWKS key material and token source locations used to validate JSON Web Tokens on a zone.
- [TokenValidationRule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tokenvalidation/tokenvalidationrule) — An API Shield JWT validation rule — selects operations/hosts on a zone and enforces a token validation expression with a `log` or `block` action.
- [TotalTls](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/acm/totaltls) — The Total TLS setting of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/acm/total_tls`).
- [Tunnel](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/tunnel) — A Cloudflare Tunnel that establishes a secure connection from your origin to Cloudflare's edge.
- [TunnelConfiguration](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/tunnelconfiguration) — Routing configuration for a remotely-managed Cloudflare Tunnel.
- [TunnelHostnameRoute](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/tunnelhostnameroute) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust **hostname route** — routes traffic for a private hostname through a `cfd_tunnel`, so WARP clients can reach internal apps by name without publishing a public DNS record.
- [TunnelRead](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/tunnelread) — Binding that lets a Worker read Cloudflare Tunnels at runtime.
- [TunnelReadWrite](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/tunnelreadwrite) — Binding that lets a Worker perform the full Cloudflare Tunnel CRUD surface at runtime.
- [TunnelRoute](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/tunnelroute) — A Cloudflare Tunnel Route attaches a private CIDR to a `cfd_tunnel` so that WARP clients (and other Zero Trust egress paths) can reach private IPs through the tunnel.
- [TunnelVirtualNetwork](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/tunnelvirtualnetwork) — A Cloudflare Zero Trust Virtual Network — an isolated routing namespace for Cloudflare Tunnel private networks.
- [TunnelWarpConnector](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/tunnelwarpconnector) — A Cloudflare WARP Connector tunnel — a software site-to-site connector that extends a private network into Cloudflare Zero Trust without running `cloudflared`.
- [TunnelWrite](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tunnel/tunnelwrite) — Binding that lets a Worker create, update, and delete Cloudflare Tunnels at runtime.
- [TurnstileWidget](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/turnstile/turnstilewidget) — A Cloudflare Turnstile widget — Cloudflare's CAPTCHA alternative.
- [UaRule](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/firewall/uarule) — A Cloudflare User Agent Blocking rule — block or challenge every request to a zone whose `User-Agent` header exactly matches a given string.
- [UniversalSsl](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/ssl/universalssl) — The Universal SSL setting of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/ssl/universal/settings`).
- [UrlNormalization](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/urlnormalization/urlnormalization) — The URL normalization configuration of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/url_normalization`) — a zone-scoped **singleton** controlling how Cloudflare normalizes incoming URLs before rule matching and before forwarding to the origin.
- [UserApiToken](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/apitoken/userapitoken) — A Cloudflare user-owned API token (`POST /user/tokens`).
- [Variants](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/cache/variants) — The Variants cache setting of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/cache/variants`).
- [VectorizeIndex](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vectorize/vectorizeindex) — A Cloudflare Vectorize index for storing and querying vector embeddings.
- [VectorizeMetadataIndex](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vectorize/vectorizemetadataindex) — A metadata index on a Cloudflare Vectorize index.
- [VersionMetadata](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/versionmetadata) — A Cloudflare Workers Version Metadata binding.
- [Vite](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/website/vite) — A Cloudflare Worker deployed from a Vite project.
- [VpcService](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vpcservice/vpcservice) — A Cloudflare VPC service that exposes a private host (IP or hostname) reachable through a Cloudflare Tunnel for Workers VPC.
- [VpcServiceRef](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vpcservice/vpcserviceref) — Reference an existing Cloudflare VPC service without managing its lifecycle.
- [VulnScannerCredential](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vulnerabilityscanner/vulnscannercredential) — A credential inside a Cloudflare Vulnerability Scanner credential set — an HTTP header or cookie value the DAST scanner attaches to outgoing requests so it can scan authenticated surfaces.
- [VulnScannerCredentialSet](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vulnerabilityscanner/vulnscannercredentialset) — A Cloudflare Vulnerability Scanner credential set — a named container for authentication credentials (headers/cookies) the DAST scanner attaches to outgoing requests when scanning authenticated surfaces.
- [VulnScannerTargetEnvironment](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/vulnerabilityscanner/vulnscannertargetenvironment) — A Cloudflare Vulnerability Scanner target environment — declares which zone the DAST-style web vulnerability scanner (Security Center, beta) is allowed to scan.
- [WaitingRoom](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/waitingroom/waitingroom) — A Cloudflare Waiting Room — places visitors in a virtual queue when traffic to a host + path exceeds the configured thresholds, protecting the origin from overload.
- [WaitingRoomSettings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/waitingroom/waitingroomsettings) — Zone-wide Cloudflare Waiting Room settings (`/zones/{zone_id}/waiting_rooms/settings`).
- [Web3Hostname](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/web3/web3hostname) — A Cloudflare Web3 gateway hostname — serve Ethereum or IPFS content from a hostname on your zone via Cloudflare's distributed web gateways.
- [Web3HostnameContentList](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/web3/web3hostnamecontentlist) — The IPFS content-blocking list of a Web3 universal-path gateway hostname — blocks specific CIDs or content paths from being served.
- [Worker](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/worker) — A Cloudflare Worker host with deploy-time binding support and runtime export collection.
- [WorkerLoader](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/workerloader) — Load and run ephemeral Workers at runtime from inline JavaScript modules.
- [WorkerRoute](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/workerroute) — A Workers Route — a zone-level mapping from a URL pattern to a Worker script.
- [WorkersAccountSetting](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/workersaccountsetting) — The account-wide Workers settings singleton (`/accounts/{account_id}/workers/account-settings`): the default usage model for new Workers and the Green Compute flag for scheduled Workers.
- [WorkersSubdomain](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/workerssubdomain) — The account-wide `workers.dev` subdomain singleton (`/accounts/{account_id}/workers/subdomain`). Every Worker with `workers.dev` enabled is served at `https://<script>.<subdomain>.workers.dev`.
- [Workflow](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/workers/workflow) — A Cloudflare Workflow that orchestrates durable, multi-step tasks with automatic retries and at-least-once delivery.
- [ZarazConfig](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/zaraz/zarazconfig) — A Cloudflare Zaraz zone configuration.
- [Zone](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/zone/zone) — A Cloudflare Zone (DNS domain) managed by Alchemy.
- [ZoneCustomNameservers](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/zone/zonecustomnameservers) — Controls whether a Cloudflare zone uses **account-level custom nameservers** (ACNS, `/zones/{zone_id}/custom_ns`).
- [ZoneDnsSettings](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/zonednssettings) — The DNS settings of a Cloudflare zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/dns_settings`) — nameserver assignment, NS TTL, SOA components, CNAME flattening, multi-provider mode, and zone mode.
- [ZoneHold](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/zone/zonehold) — A Cloudflare zone hold (`/zones/{zone_id}/hold`) — prevents the zone's hostname (and optionally its subdomains) from being added to another Cloudflare account while the hold is active.
- [ZoneResourceTags](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/tags/zoneresourcetags) — Key/value tags attached to a zone-level Cloudflare resource via the unified resource-tagging API (open beta).
- [ZoneSetting](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/zone/zonesetting) — A single Cloudflare zone setting (`/zones/{zone_id}/settings/{settingId}`) pinned to a desired value.
- [ZoneTransferAcl](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/zonetransferacl) — A Secondary DNS zone-transfer ACL (`/accounts/{account_id}/secondary_dns/acls`) — an account-wide IPv4/IPv6 range that may receive NOTIFYs for secondary zones and from which Cloudflare accepts AXFR/IXFR requests for outgoing transfers.
- [ZoneTransferIncoming](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/zonetransferincoming) — The incoming zone-transfer configuration of a secondary zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/secondary_dns/incoming`) — links the zone to the {@link ZoneTransferPeer | peers} Cloudflare transfers it in from and sets the auto-refresh interval.
- [ZoneTransferOutgoing](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/zonetransferoutgoing) — The outgoing zone-transfer configuration of a primary zone (`/zones/{zone_id}/secondary_dns/outgoing`) — links the zone to the {@link ZoneTransferPeer | peers} Cloudflare NOTIFYs and serves AXFR/IXFR to, and toggles transfers on or off via the dedicated enable/disable endpoints.
- [ZoneTransferPeer](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/zonetransferpeer) — A Secondary DNS zone-transfer peer (`/accounts/{account_id}/secondary_dns/peers`) — an external nameserver Cloudflare exchanges zone transfers with. Link peers to a zone via {@link ZoneTransferIncoming} (secondary zones) or {@link ZoneTransferOutgoing} (primary zones).
- [ZoneTransferTsig](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/cloudflare/dns/zonetransfertsig) — A Secondary DNS TSIG key (`/accounts/{account_id}/secondary_dns/tsigs`) — shared-secret authentication for zone transfers between Cloudflare and external nameservers. Reference it from a {@link ZoneTransferPeer} via `tsigId`.

### Command

- [Build](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/command/build) — A `Build` runs a shell command that produces an output asset (a file or directory) and tracks that asset in state. Unlike `Exec`, a `Build` has an output contract: `reconcile` verifies the command actually produced `outdir` and exposes its location so downstream resources (e.g. a `Cloudflare.Worker`'s static assets) can consume it.
- [Dev](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/command/dev) — A long-lived shell process scoped to a stack instance, started during `alchemy dev` and restarted when its inputs change. During `alchemy deploy` this is a no-op — `Dev` resources only run in dev mode.
- [Exec](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/command/exec) — An `Exec` runs a shell command purely for its side effects — it has no output contract. Unlike `Build`, it does not produce or track an output asset; `reconcile` runs the command and the resource succeeds as long as the command exits with code `0` (a non-zero exit fails with a `CommandError`).

### Drizzle

- [postgres](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/drizzle/postgres) — Open a Drizzle/Postgres database from a connection URL using the `drizzle-orm/effect-postgres` integration.
- [Schema](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/drizzle/schema) — A Drizzle schema managed as an Alchemy resource.

### GitHub

- [Comment](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/comment) — A GitHub issue or pull request comment.
- [events](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/events) — Subscribe to events emitted by a GitHub repository.
- [Repository](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/repository) — A GitHub repository.
- [Secret](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/secret) — A GitHub Actions repository or environment secret.
- [Secrets](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/secrets) — Bulk-creates a set of {@link Secret}s in the same repository (and optionally the same environment).
- [Variable](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/variable) — A GitHub Actions repository variable.
- [Variables](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/variables) — Bulk-creates a set of {@link Variable}s in the same repository.
- [Webhook](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/github/webhook) — A GitHub repository webhook.

### Kubernetes

- [ConfigMap](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/kubernetes/configmap) — A Kubernetes config map bound to an EKS cluster.
- [Deployment](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/kubernetes/deployment) — A Kubernetes deployment bound to an EKS cluster.
- [Job](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/kubernetes/job) — A Kubernetes job bound to an EKS cluster.
- [Namespace](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/kubernetes/namespace) — A Kubernetes namespace bound to an EKS cluster.
- [Service](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/kubernetes/service) — A Kubernetes service bound to an EKS cluster.
- [ServiceAccount](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/kubernetes/serviceaccount) — A Kubernetes service account bound to an EKS cluster.

### Neon

- [Branch](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/neon/branch) — A branch of a Neon project.
- [Project](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/neon/project) — A Neon serverless Postgres project.

### Planetscale

- [MySQLBranch](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/mysql/mysqlbranch) — A PlanetScale branch of a {@link MySQLDatabase}. For PostgreSQL branches use {@link PostgresBranch} instead.
- [MySQLDatabase](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/mysql/mysqldatabase) — A MySQL PlanetScale database (powered by Vitess). For PostgreSQL use {@link PostgresDatabase} instead.
- [MySQLPassword](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/mysql/mysqlpassword) — A PlanetScale password for accessing a MySQL database branch.
- [PostgresBranch](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/postgres/postgresbranch) — A PlanetScale branch of a {@link PostgresDatabase}. For MySQL branches use {@link MySQLBranch} instead.
- [PostgresDatabase](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/postgres/postgresdatabase) — A PostgreSQL PlanetScale database. For MySQL, use {@link MySQLDatabase} instead.
- [PostgresDefaultRole](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/postgres/postgresdefaultrole) — The default PlanetScale PostgreSQL role for a database branch.
- [PostgresRole](https://v2.alchemy.run/providers/planetscale/postgres/postgresrole) — A PlanetScale role for accessing a PostgreSQL database branch.
